title: Tender
text:
This is the frightening one.

Not because of what happens in it. Because of how it feels.

The dream is quiet. She is there the way she has been in every dream — present, observant, the weight of her attention now familiar in the way that returning somewhere familiar is, which is to say that you recognize it without having agreed to recognize it.

But she is different here. The quality of her attention is different. There is no assessment in it. There is no satisfaction at a project proceeding as designed. There is something else, something that has no proper name in the context of what she has been doing to you over these months, which is: care.

She is careful with you. In the dream she is careful with you in the way that someone is careful with something they intend to keep.

She says something. The dream, for once, lets you hear it. You will not be writing it down. You will not be telling the others. You will carry it in the specific interior place where things live that are too true to be examined in daylight.

You wake before dawn. Saoirse is on watch. She looks at you once and then back to the tree line, which means she saw something in your face and has elected to let you have it in private.

The road continues. You continue. The word she used in the dream — you know what it was. You are choosing, for now, not to know.
